---
pagetitle: "OpenPipeline, modular single-cell & spatial pipelines"
description: "Modular, containerized components and reproducible Nextflow workflows for single-cell and spatial data."
toc: false
page-layout: full
anchor-sections: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="op-home">

  <div class="wrap hero">
    <span class="kicker">Multi-modal omics analysis · reproducible at scale</span>
    <h1>Modular, reproducible pipelines for<br><em>single-cell and spatial</em> data.</h1>
    <p class="lede">Modular, containerized components and reproducible Nextflow workflows: from raw counts to a processed, interpretable data object.</p>
    <div class="modalities">
      <span class="m"><b>RNA</b></span>
      <span class="m"><b>Protein</b> (ADT)</span>
      <span class="m"><b>ATAC</b></span>
      <span class="m"><b>VDJ</b></span>
      <span class="m"><b>GDO</b></span>
      <span class="m"><b>Spatial</b></span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="wrap">
    <div id="op-flow"></div>
    <div class="cta-row flow-cta">
      <a class="btn primary" href="concepts/architecture.html">▸ Full workflow overview</a>
    </div>
  </div>

  <section class="block alt">
    <div class="wrap">
      <div class="sec-head">
        <span class="kicker">Why OpenPipeline</span>
        <h2>What makes it different</h2>
        <p>Design choices that set OpenPipeline apart from a typical analysis pipeline.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="features">
        <div class="feature">
          <b>True modularity</b>
          <p>Modular not just in code but at the execution level: every step is an independently built, containerized unit you can run, swap, reorder, or reuse as a dependency across packages.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="feature">
          <b>Standard multi-omics data object</b>
          <p>All modalities, RNA, protein, ATAC, spatial, and more, share one standard data object (MuData), so any component works with any other.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="feature">
          <b>Reproducible</b>
          <p>Containers pin each component's exact dependencies and every release is versioned, so the same run gives the same results today and years from now.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="feature">
          <b>Scalable &amp; portable</b>
          <p>Built on Nextflow, the same pipeline runs unchanged from a laptop to an HPC cluster or the cloud, in parallel across samples.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="feature">
          <b>Best practices by default</b>
          <p>Software-engineering rigor brought to bioinformatics, following the single-cell community's analysis best practices.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="feature">
          <b>Community-driven, team-maintained</b>
          <p>Open source and developed in the open, but not a release-once academic project: actively maintained for years by a dedicated team.</p>
        </div>
      </div>
      <p class="ways-note">The <a href="concepts/index.html">concepts</a> pages explain how each of these work.</p>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="block">
    <div class="wrap">
      <div class="sec-head">
        <span class="kicker">Where to start</span>
        <h2>Three ways in</h2>
        <p>Three entry points, from running a workflow as-is, to assembling your own analysis, to tuning every parameter.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="cards3">
        <a class="rcard link" href="get-started/index.html">
          <div class="tag">🧪 New to single-cell</div>
          <h3>Guided first run</h3>
          <p>Run a best-practice pipeline on provided test data and learn to read the output, no prior Nextflow knowledge needed.</p>
          <span class="go">Get started →</span>
        </a>
        <a class="rcard link" href="guides/index.html">
          <div class="tag">🔬 Bioinformatician</div>
          <h3>Task recipes</h3>
          <p>Pick and choose the data processing steps needed for your analysis, and learn to optimize your runs.</p>
          <span class="go">Browse guides →</span>
        </a>
        <a class="rcard link" href="reference/index.html">
          <div class="tag">⚡ Power user</div>
          <h3>Component reference</h3>
          <p>Every workflow and component, grouped by task, with full parameter listings.</p>
          <span class="go">Open reference →</span>
        </a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="block alt">
    <div class="wrap">
      <div class="sec-head">
        <span class="kicker">Run it your way</span>
        <h2>Local, cloud, or no-code</h2>
        <p>Every workflow is a <b>Viash</b>-built, containerized Nextflow pipeline, so the same run works on your laptop, on Seqera, or through the Viash Hub web interface.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="ways">
        <div class="way featured">
          <span class="way-flag">Recommended</span>
          <div class="h"><a href="https://www.viash-hub.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Viash Hub</a> <span class="badge">no-code</span></div>
          <p>Fill in a guided web form, no YAML, and it hands you ready-to-run instructions for the Local or Seqera routes.</p>
          <div class="req">requires: a browser</div>
        </div>
        <div class="way">
          <div class="h">Local <span class="badge">self-run</span></div>
          <p>Run on your own machine, an HPC cluster, or a cloud VM with more compute, using Nextflow and Docker.</p>
          <div class="req">requires: nextflow · docker</div>
        </div>
        <div class="way">
          <div class="h"><a href="https://seqera.io" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Seqera Platform</a> <span class="badge">managed</span></div>
          <p>Launch and monitor runs on managed compute, cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP), Kubernetes, or an HPC scheduler, from the Seqera UI or CLI.</p>
          <div class="req">requires: a Seqera compute environment</div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <p class="ways-note">Step-by-step how-tos for each route live in the <a href="guides/index.html">guides</a>.</p>
    </div>
  </section>

</div>
```

```{=html}
<script src="flow.js"></script>
```
